title: [lichen_l2.c:507-528] peer_try_all_pubkeys constant-time claim is weak
description:
Round 2 gap analysis: Missed because the constant-time comment looked plausible.

At lines 498-528:
```c
/*
 * SECURITY: Constant-time peer iteration to prevent timing side-channel.
 *
 * Always iterate through ALL peers even after finding a match. This
 * prevents an attacker from inferring which peer index matched based
 * on how quickly the function returns.
 */
int found_idx = -1;
int found_ret = -LICHEN_EAUTH;

for (size_t i = 0; i < CONFIG_LICHEN_LINK_MAX_NEIGHBORS; i++) {
    if (!peer_table[i].active) {
        continue;  // <-- NOT constant-time: skip inactive peers
    }
    ...
```

The comment claims constant-time iteration, but:
1. Inactive peers are skipped (line 508-510), making iteration time depend on active peer count
2. lichen_link_rx() itself is NOT constant-time (signature verification has data-dependent timing)
3. Non-auth errors abort early (line 536)

The 'constant-time' claim is only partially true - it's constant relative to WHICH peer matches (index position), but NOT constant relative to total active peers or signature verification cost.

This isn't necessarily a vulnerability (L2 timing attacks over LoRa are impractical), but the SECURITY comment overstates the protection. A more accurate description would be 'peer-index-independent iteration'.
close_reason: Fixed: Updated comment to accurately describe protection as peer-index-independent iteration with documented limitations
